cards.title: Creating Meaningful Rituals With Your Child
cards.body: A mother shares how she created a special high tea tradition with her daughter to mark an important milestone. Rituals can help children feel seen and create lasting memories during pivotal moments in their development.

cards.title: Creativity Thrives When You Become a Parent
cards.body: A parent discovers that having kids actually expanded his creative perspective rather than limiting it. Children's imaginative observations—like calling kale stalks 'giraffes'—can inspire fresh artistic thinking.

cards.title: Supporting Sensitive Teens Through Social Pressure
cards.body: Experts advise parents of sensitive adolescents struggling with peer comparison and social media anxiety. As children mature, their social world naturally expands, helping them find their tribe.

cards.title: Why Forced Apologies Backfire With Children
cards.body: Forcing children to apologize is a common parenting tactic that may actually undermine learning and emotional growth. A more effective approach involves helping kids understand the impact of their actions naturally.

cards.title: Countdown Strategy for Better Child Behavior
cards.body: Counting down is a proven parenting technique that helps children transition between activities smoothly. This simple strategy gives kids time to mentally prepare and reduces power struggles.

cards.title: Getting Resistant Teens Into Therapy
cards.body: When teens resist therapy, curiosity works better than arguments, according to Dialectical Behavior Therapy principles. Meeting adolescents where they are emotionally opens the door to professional support.

cards.title: Should Teens Disclose Neurodivergence in Essays
cards.body: A former college admissions consultant weighs the pros and cons of revealing neurodivergence in applications. The decision depends on how the teen frames their experience and institutional fit.
